prompt: Create a tall vertical travel poster in a refined monochrome architectural line-art style on warm ivory paper, with delicate sepia-brown ink outlines and very light beige shading. The scene is a pedestrian street in Chengdu Taikoo Li, China, drawn with precise thin lines like a hand-rendered urban sketch: traditional Daci Temple architecture with ornate curved tiled roofs and bamboo on the left, modern glass retail buildings with balconies and storefronts on the right, a long paved promenade receding in one-point perspective, dense but lightly drawn crowds walking and sitting at outdoor café tables, bicycles, planters, trees, umbrellas, and distant high-rise towers with a slender landmark tower centered in the skyline. At the top, place a large elegant Chinese headline 成都太古里 in dark reddish-brown serif calligraphy, with a smaller centered subtitle below reading 中国 · 四川 · 成都 flanked by thin horizontal lines. Include exactly 3 visible sign text elements in the street scene: a vertical stone sign in the lower left reading 大慈寺 with small English “Daci Temple LI” beneath it, a hanging vertical shop sign on the right reading 成都太古里 with small English “TAIKOO LI” beneath it, and a traditional temple plaque on the left gate reading 古大慈寺. Keep the composition airy with large blank space in the upper half, no heavy colors, no photorealism, no thick outlines, no messy hatching, and no extra title text beyond the specified signs.
